verified_properties: strict full ratio cone at indices 1,2,3
strictly increasing scaled defects below one
both reciprocal-defect increments strictly between zero and one
both neighboring cubic Jensen frontier polynomials strictly negative
contiguous order-three signed-Hankel determinant has the wrong positive sign

defect_coordinate.interpretation: The defect sequence may be log-convex, but its centered log-curvature must stay below the explicit buffer -2*log x_(n+2).

determinant_coordinate.expected_sign: The order-three signed-Hankel condition is D_(3,n)<0.

forward_invariance_theorem.boundary: Complete shifted contiguous order three only; noncontiguous order-three minors are not included.
forward_invariance_theorem.conclusion: C_n(lambda)>0 for every n>=0 and finite lambda>=-100
forward_invariance_theorem.statement: The exact cooperative system C_n'/r_(n+2)=alpha_n*C_(n+1)+beta_n*C_n, the cubic forward-uniform tail, and a weighted maximum principle propagate C_n>0 through lambda=0.

live_handoff.forbidden_promotion: The full ratio cone, decreasing defect, increasing scaled defect, and strict cubic Jensen increment bounds do not imply C_n>0 without the lambda=-100 scaled-defect anchor used by the entry theorem.
live_handoff.target: Find coordinates, entry theorems, and forward-invariance laws for contiguous order four; then transfer to arbitrary columns and continue to higher compound orders.

m100_entry_theorem.boundary: All shifts at lambda=-100, but only the contiguous order-three family.
m100_entry_theorem.statement: A repaired Arb prefix through n=317 and an exact scaled-defect tail prove C_n(-100)>0 for every n>=0.

noncontiguous_transfer_theorem.boundary: Complete reshaped-Hankel order three, not order four.
noncontiguous_transfer_theorem.conclusion: R_(3,n)(j_1,j_2,j_3)<0 for every n>=0 and 0<=j_1<j_2<j_3 at lambda=0
noncontiguous_transfer_theorem.statement: Positive column scaling embeds the three-row Hankel columns as planar points. Contiguous negativity makes their edge slopes strictly decrease, and secant averaging transfers the sign to every strictly increasing column triple.

reciprocal_defect_coordinate.interpretation: This is unit-buffered log-concavity of the reciprocal defects.

sufficient_increment_theorem.scope: This is sufficient only. Actual reciprocal-defect increments may decrease.
sufficient_increment_theorem.strictness: C_n>0 unless a_n=b_n=1; the strict cubic cone excludes that equality.

proof_boundary: This artifact factors the contiguous 3x3 signed-Hankel minor into contraction, defect, reciprocal-defect, and increment coordinates; proves one sufficient increment theorem; and supplies a strict rational countermodel to promotion from the already proved ratio, adaptive-defect, and cubic Jensen cones; and composes the all-shift entry, forward-invariance, and noncontiguous secant-transfer theorems. It does not prove compound order four or higher, the all-order signed-Hankel antecedent, the Jensen bridge, RH, or Lambda<=0.
